source.detail: Closed-form Nan-EMT effective conductivity of the reference DGEBA epoxy + 5 vol% graphene-nanoplatelet draft from the materialscodegraph composite tool (mcg/tools/composite/emt.py effective_kappa, ported verbatim to frontend/src/lib/emt.ts): matrix km=0.2, filler k11=1200 (in-plane) and k33=6 (through-plane) W/(m K), platelet d1=5um and d3=0.02um (aspect d3/d1=0.004, oblate), interface conductance G=25 MW/(m^2 K), volume fraction f=0.05, randomly oriented. Kapitza radius a_K=km/G=8.0 nm. Pinned at kappa_random=1.2452 W/(m K) by the mcg eval target (mcg/evals/paper_targets.yaml composite_kappa.kappa_random_W_per_mK, tolerance 0.001), cross-pinned in frontend/src/lib/emt.test.ts and mcg/tests/tools/composite/test_emt.py. Validated formulas: C.-W. Nan, R. Birringer, D. R. Clarke, H. Gleiter, Effective thermal conductivity of particulate composites with interfacial thermal resistance, J. Appl. Phys. 81, 6692 (1997); spherical-limit cross-check D. P. H. Hasselman, L. F. Johnson, Effective thermal conductivity of composites with interfacial thermal barrier resistance, J. Compos. Mater. 21, 508 (1987).
